title: Next-gen spacecraft are overwhelming communication networks
SpaceX's 1 million satellites could avoid environmental checks
summary: The environmental impact of SpaceX's planned gargantuan mega-constellation is still being grappled with, but the FCC isn’t required to study it

title: The Human Flatus Atlas plans to measure the explosivity of farts
summary: Feedback is excited to learn that University of Maryland researchers are measuring farts in a bid to build a Human Flatus Atlas, a project that seems destined for an Ig Nobel

title: Amazing sneak peek of NASA's spacesuit tests as moon mission nears
summary: NASA crew members practise emergency rescue drills in a 40-foot-deep pool simulating the lunar surface, as part of tests on a new generation of spacesuit, the Axiom Extravehicular Mobility Unit

title: Loophole found that makes quantum cloning possible
summary: Duplicating the information held in quantum computers was thought to be impossible thanks to the no-cloning theorem, but researchers have now found a workaround

title: Atmospheric pollution caused by space junk could be a huge problem
summary: After a Falcon 9 rocket stage burned up in the atmosphere, vaporised lithium and other metals drifted over Europe. This growing type of pollution could destroy ozone and form climate-warming clouds

title: Artists gaze into space in stunning new exhibition
summary: A new show at the Royal West of England Academy brings together a series of works that interweave art and science

title: Why some people cannot move on from the death of a loved one
summary: Prolonged grief disorder affects around 1 in 20 people, and we're starting to understand the neuroscience behind it

title: Backwards heat shows laws of thermodynamics may need a quantum update
summary: We are used to heat flowing from hot objects to cool ones, and never the other way round, but now researchers have found it is possible to pull off this trick in the strange realm of quantum mechanics

title: Why self-expansion is the key to long-lasting love and friendship
summary: A growing body of psychological research shows that the best relationships – romantic or otherwise – come with a feeling of personal growth. Columnist David Robson explores the evidence-backed ways to broaden our horizons and connect more deeply with our loves, our friends and ourselves
